english: par ston dgos (par ston dgos) you must tell me minutely how she looks, what kind of appearance she has Glr.; p'ug c'i _dra c'ig oṅ (phug ci 'dra cig 'ong) what will be the upshot? where is this to end? Glr.; ṅa ji-_dra-bar de bzin _gyur (nga ji 'dra bar de bzhin 'gyur) he becomes just what I am Stg.; *yhán-dé* (*yhan de*) C. col. how? _dra-_dra ('dra 'dra) (W. *dán-da* (*dan da*)) very frq. for _drá-ba ('dra ba), e.g. śa-dkár-gyi rgyu _drá-_dra-la tig-rtse-zer (sha dkar gyi rgyu 'dra 'dra la tig rtse zer) something similar to the substance of tin is called zinc; _dra mi _dra ('dra mi 'dra) like and unlike; equality, likeness, similarity, _dra mi _dra ltá-ba ('dra mi 'dra lta ba) to examine the likeness Glr. -- 2. sbst. 1. resemblance, likeness, v. _dógs-pa ('dogs pa) 2. -- 2. form, shape, appearance, phase. Thg.
pf. dral (dral) (cf. rál-ba (ral ba) and hrál-ba (hral ba)), to tear to pieces, to rend asunder; also to pull down, a house; to rip up, to cut open, an animal.

pf. and imp. _dris ('dris), 1. to ask, ... la (la), W. nas (nas), a person; with accus. to enquire after or about a thing; grós-_dri-sa (gros 'dri sa) a place for asking advice, oracle Glr.; blá-ma _drí-ba (bla ma 'dri ba) to inquire after one's Lama Mil.; p'a-mái ytam (pha ma'i gtam) after one's parents Dzl.; _drí-bai tsig ('dri ba'i tshig) interrogative pronoun, e.g. c'i (ci) Gram.; v. also drí-ba (dri ba). -- 2. inst. of _brí-ba ('bri ba).
Glr. fol. 57, 12? another reading: ldiṅ-ba (lding ba).

pf. dril (dril), I. vb. n., cf. _gril-ba ('gril ba) and hril-ba (hril ba), 1. to be turned, rolled round or twisted into a thing, od-zér-gyi gáṅ-bur ('od zer gyi gang bur) to be wrapped into a covering of light Glr.; to gather, to flow together, as p'ó-bai bád-kan (pho ba'i bad kan), the gastric phlegm Med.; fig.: blo-séms yćig-tu _dril-te (blo sems gcig tu 'dril te) whilst our minds were flowing together Glr.; yúl-pa-rnams ká-_dril-te ṅó-log-pa (yul pa rnams ka 'dril te ngo log pa) a conspiracy Schr. -- 2. to roll down, ri-bo ṅos-la (ri bo ngos la) the slope of a hill Thgy. -- 3. to fall, to fall down W. II. vb. a., cf. sgríl-ba (sgril ba), to wrap up, rás-kyis (ras kyis) in a kandkerchief Glr.; dar sna lṅas (dar sna lngas) in five sorts of silk Glr.; zaṅs-kyis (zangs kyis) (covered or sheathed) with copper Mil.; to heap together, to pile up, mé-tog puṅ-por dril (me tog phung por dril) the blossoms are aggregated, heaped together in a panicle Wdn.; dril-bas (dril bas) in short, to sum up all, in summa Glr. -- ljags _dril-ba (ljags 'dril ba) Sch.: to play with the tongue, moving it to and fro.

to be accustomed to, to be acquainted with, gen. with daṅ (dang), Glr. and col.; rarely with accus.: ynyen ji tsam _dris bzin (gnyen ji tsam 'dris bzhin) the more friends you get familiar with; mig _dris c'és-na (mig 'dris ches na) if persons constantly see one another, get perfectly used to one another, Mil.; mostly adj. (= góms-pa (goms pa)) accustomed, used, mi (mi) or k'áṅ-pa daṅ (khang pa dang), to men, to one's house; also dris-pa (dris pa) used absol. = tame W.; dris-pa mi (dris pa mi) an acquaintance, a sympathizing friend, an assistant Thgy.; sṅar-_dris-kyi mi (sngar 'dris kyi mi) an old acquaintance, an old crony Thgr. A derivation of dris-pa (dris pa) from _drid-pa ('drid pa), _brid-pa ('brid pa), to deceive, to bait, to decoy, and hence to tame, was suggested by some Tibetans, but is after all scarcely to be authenticated.
to fall into small pieces, to crumble (away) Sch.

pf. and imp. drud (drud) (_drus? ('drus?)), rarely _brúd-pa ('brud pa), 1. to rub, lus (lus) the body; to file, to rasp, śiṅ (shing) wood, Lex.; to rub off, to scour, *bé-ma daṅ* (*be ma dang*) W.; to polish, to smooth, to plane, p'ag-ste (phag ste) with a plane W.; to grind, to powder, to pulverize (?). -- 2. to drag, to draw or pull along on the ground, by a rope, ro sá-la (ro sa la) a dead body on the ground (ma-_drús-par (ma 'drus par) without slipping (?) Med.) -- 3. *dúd-de gyur toṅ* (*dud de gyur tong*) W. move, or push it a little aside; dud ćàd-će (dud cad ce) W. to cut off obliquely (?).
pf. and imp. drub (drub) (s) 1. to sew Sch., so perh. Dzl. 22o, 11.
